question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractFunctionResult containing crafted log entries with arbitrary topic0/topic1/topic2/topic3 values, causing the stored ContractLog records to contain attacker-controlled event topics that mislead event-subscription clients monitoring specific contract events?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractFunctionResult containing a large number of log entries, causing processContractLogs() to insert an unbounded number of ContractLog records, exhausting database storage and causing a denial-of-service against the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction whose ContractFunctionResult log entries reference a contractId different from the executing contract, causing processContractLogs() to store ContractLog records attributed to an arbitrary contract, poisoning that contract's event history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractFunctionResult containing crafted log entries with arbitrary topic0/topic1/topic2/topic3 values, causing the stored ContractLog records to contain attacker-controlled event topics that mislead event-subscription clients monitoring specific contract events?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a ContractFunctionResult containing a large number of log entries, causing processContractLogs() to insert an unbounded number of ContractLog records, exhausting database storage and causing a denial-of-service against the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractLogs()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with crafted log data containing oversized bloom bytes, causing processContractLogs() to store an arbitrarily large bloom field in the contract_log table, leading to storage exhaustion or column overflow errors?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractAction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar ContractAction records containing a crafted callingAccount or callingContract that resolves to an arbitrary EntityId, causing processContractAction() to store a ContractAction attributed to an account the attacker does not control, falsifying the call trace?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractAction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar ContractAction records containing a crafted recipientAddress (targeted_address), causing processContractAction() to store an arbitrary byte sequence as the recipient address, enabling address spoofing in the contract action trace?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractAction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar ContractAction records containing a manipulated callDepth value of 0, causing processContractAction() to treat a non-top-level action as the top-level action, corrupting the gas accounting in SidecarProcessingResult.topLevelActionSidecarGasUsed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractAction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar ContractAction records containing an oversized input field, causing processContractAction() to store an arbitrarily large byte array in the contract_action.input column, exhausting database storage?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractStateChange()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with sidecar ContractStateChange records referencing a contractId that belongs to a different contract than the one being executed, causing processContractStateChange() to attribute storage changes to an arbitrary contract, poisoning its state history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processContractStateChange()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a large number of sidecar ContractStateChange records, causing processContractStateChange() to insert an unbounded number of rows into the contract_state_change table, exhausting database storage and causing a denial-of-service?
